findings.title: Dev fee is double-counted, minting reflected balance to the contract on every taxed transfer

best_agent_match.title: Dev-fee tokens are minted from thin air on every taxed transfer

findings.title: Cooldown is keyed by `sender`, enabling a market-wide 60 second buy denial of service

best_agent_match.title: Cooldown is keyed by sender, causing a global 60-second buy denial of service

findings.title: Owner can blacklist the LP pair and freeze all trading

best_agent_match.title: Owner can blacklist the LP pair and permanently freeze all trading

findings.title: Ownership can be made to look renounced and later reclaimed

best_agent_match.title: Ownership can be made to look renounced and later reclaimed

findings.title: Forced full-balance auto-swaps use `amountOutMin = 0`, making them trivially sandwichable

best_agent_match.title: Forced full-balance auto-swaps use `amountOutMin = 0`, making them trivially sandwichable

findings.title: A reverting or gas-heavy team wallet can turn auto-swap into a sell-side denial of service

best_agent_match.title: A bad team wallet can turn auto-swap into a sell-side denial of service

rejected_candidates.title: Owner can drain all tokens via manualSwap
rejected_candidates.reason: `manualSwap()` only swaps tokens already held by the contract from fees; it does not let the owner seize arbitrary user balances. This is an admin fee-handling function, not a separate exploit.

rejected_candidates.title: Owner can change charity wallet to steal funds
rejected_candidates.reason: Changing the fee recipient is an explicit owner-controlled parameter of the fee system. By itself this is centralization/trust risk, not an unintended vulnerability distinct from the retained-admin findings.

rejected_candidates.title: Sniper list can be abused to block any address
rejected_candidates.reason: Overstated as written: `_transfer()` checks `recipient` and `msg.sender`, not `sender`, so a blacklisted holder can still move tokens out via `transferFrom` through a non-blacklisted spender such as the router. The stronger pair-freeze variant is retained separately.

rejected_candidates.title: Transaction limits can be fully removed by owner
rejected_candidates.reason: `_maxTxAmount` is never enforced anywhere in `_transfer()`, so changing or removing it has no effect and does not introduce a new exploit path.

rejected_candidates.title: UniswapOnly restriction can be disabled
rejected_candidates.reason: `_removeDestLimit()` is an explicit owner toggle for an anti-bot restriction. Disabling that restriction is an intended admin action, not a standalone vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Reflection rate calculation may underflow
rejected_candidates.reason: The cited `rSupply < _rTotal.div(_tTotal)` check is the standard RFI-style fallback guard in `_getCurrentSupply()`. No concrete underflow or exploitable mis-accounting was demonstrated from this condition itself.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing event logs for critical functions
rejected_candidates.reason: Missing events are a transparency issue, but not a realistic protocol-level vulnerability causing theft, insolvency, lockup, or denial of service.

rejected_candidates.title: Division in reflection calculation may lose precision
rejected_candidates.reason: Integer truncation on fee calculations is expected Solidity behavior and does not create meaningful exploitable harm here.

rejected_candidates.title: Block timestamp can be manipulated by miner
rejected_candidates.reason: This is a generic blockchain property and does not materially change the already-retained cooldown flaw or create a separate reportable issue in this contract.
